content.full_text:
CITY OF HAYWARD

Office of the City Clerk

June 12, 1992

Louisa Menchaca, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0804

Dear Ms. Menchaca:

I am requesting a written confirmation of our telephone conversation of June 1, 1992, regarding use of surplus campaign funds held by a defeated candidate.

In the conversation, you stated that any surplus funds may be used as a contribution to support a local ballot measure pursuant to G.C. 89519(e). Funds are considered surplus at the end of the postelection reporting period following the defeat of the candidate. This candidate was defeated in the April 14, 1992, General Municipal Election. The last postelection reporting period ends June 30, 1992. The funds would not be surplus until that time.

Thank you for your help.

Sincerely,

Judy Vorada
City Clerk

(a) Campaign funds shall not be used to compensate a candidate or elected officer for the performance of
political, legislative, or governmental activities, except for reimbursement of out-of-pocket expenses
incurred for political, legislative, or governmental purposes.
(b) Campaign funds shall not be used to compensate any individual or individuals with authority to approve
the expenditure of campaign funds for the performance of political, legislative, or governmental activities,
except as provided in subdivision (b) of Section 89513 and for reimbursement of out-of-pocket expenses
incurred for political, legislative, or governmental purposes.

§ 89519. Surplus campaign funds.

Upon leaving any elected office, or at the end of the postelection reporting period following the defeat of a
candidate for elective office, whichever occurs last, campaign funds shall after January 1, 1994 under the
control of the former candidate or elected officer shall be considered surplus campaign funds and shall be
controlled by the former candidate or elected officer and shall only and shall be used only for the following
purposes:

or loans to bona fide charitable, educational, civic,
tions, where no substantial part of the proceeds will
ence of a candidate or an individual or
ict officer, campaign treasurer, or any individual or
e of campaign funds held by a committee, or member
the donation or that bears a reasonable relation to a

(a) The payment of outstanding campaign debts or elected officer's expenses.
(b) The pro rata repayment of contributions.
(c) Donations to any bona fide charitable, educational, civic, religious, or similar tax-exempt, nonprofit
organization, where no substantial part of the proceeds will have a material financial effect on the former
candidate or elected officer, any member of his or her immediate family, or his or her campaign treasurer.
(d) Contributions to a political party or committee so long as such funds are not used to make contributions
in support of or opposition to a candidate for elective office.
(e) Contributions to support or oppose any candidate for political office, any candidate for elective office in
a state other than California, or any ballot measure.
(f) The payment for professional services reasonably required by the committee to assist in the performance
of its administrative functions, including payment for attorney's fees for litigation which arises directly out
of a candidate's or elected officer's activities, duties, or status as a candidate or elected officer, including,
but not limited to, an action to enjoin disinformation, defense of an action brought of a violation of state or
federal law, an action for declaratory relief or injunctive relief, and an action arising from an election contest or recount.
Added Stats 1990 ch 84 § 13 (SB 1738). Amended Stats 1991 ch 546 § 9 (SB 1194).

embedding.qa_text: June 12, 1992 Louisa Menchaca, Legal Division Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street, Suite 800 Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804-0804 Dear Ms. Menchaca: I am requesting a written confirmation of our telephone conversation of June 1, 1992, regarding use of surplus campaign funds held by a defeated candidate. In the conversation, you stated that any surplus funds may be used as a contribution to support a local ballot measure pursuant to G.C. 89519(e). Funds are considered surplus at the end of the postelection reporting period following the defeat of the candidate. This candidate was defeated in the April 14, 1992, General Municipal Election. The last postelection reporting period ends June 30, 1992. The funds would not be surplus until that time. Thank you for your help. Sincerely, Judy Vonada City Clerk The section of the Government Code titled "GOVERNMENT CODE" discusses the use and limitations of campaign funds, particularly in relation to vehicle expenses, real property, and compensation for political activity. Here is a summary of the key points: ### § 89521. Liability for receipt of honorarium or gift in violation of chapter Any person who makes or receives an honorarium, gift, or expenditure in violation of this chapter is liable. ### § 89517. Lease or purchase of real property (a) Campaign funds shall not be used for payment or reimbursement for the lease of real property or for the purchase, lease, or refurbishment of any appliance or equipment, where the lessee or sublessor is, or the legal title resides, in whole or in part, in a candidate, elected officer, campaign treasurer, or any individual or individuals with authority to approve the expenditure of campaign funds, or member of his or her immediate family. (b) Campaign funds shall not be used to purchase real property. Except as prohibited by subdivision (a), campaign funds may be used to lease real property for up to one year at a time where the use of that property is directly related to political, legislative, or governmental purposes. (c) For the purposes of this section, real property, appliance, or equipment is considered to be directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose as long as its use for other purposes is only incidental to its use for political, legislative, or governmental purposes. ### § 89518. Compensation of candidate for political activity (a) Campaign funds shall not be used to compensate a candidate or elected officer for the performance of political, legislative, or governmental activities, except for reimbursement of out-of-pocket expenses incurred for political, legislative, or governmental purposes. (b) Campaign funds shall not be used to compensate any individual or individuals with authority to approve the expenditure of campaign funds for the performance of political, legislative, or governmental activities, except as provided in subdivision (b) of Section 89513 and for reimbursement of out-of-pocket expenses incurred for political, legislative, or governmental purposes. ### § 89519. Surplus campaign funds Upon leaving any elected office, or at the end of the postelection reporting period following the defeat
